---
title: "Teaching"
subtitle: "Lessons, tutorials, and lecture materials"
listing:
  - id: subjects
    contents:
      - teaching/*/index.qmd
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 2
    fields: [title, subtitle, description, categories]
    sort-ui: false
    filter-ui: false
page-layout: full
title-block-banner: true
---

Materials I have written or used in teaching biostatistics, machine learning,
and clinical data science — primarily aimed at medical students, PhD candidates,
and clinical researchers at FMUP / RISE-Health.

Where it adds something, lessons are published as **live in-browser
documents**: readers can edit code, drag sliders, and re-fit models without
installing R. The R interpreter ships compiled to WebAssembly and runs entirely
client-side — no server, no account, no setup.

## Subjects

::: {#subjects}
:::
